info.name: LMS — Syllabus: Chapters & Topics (v1)
info.description:
Teacher/student APIs for the chapter + topic tree (the `content` module). The SAME endpoints back two app screens: the **Syllabus** (topics are just a name + order) and **Study Content** (topics also carry `topic_content` text, a `link` URL + an optional image / PDF). Teacher edits reflect for students and admin because they all read/write the same `chapters`/`topics` tables.

**Roles & scoping:**
- **List (POST /content/get)** — STUDENTS are auto-scoped server-side to their own class + section (they only need to send `subject_id`; any class/section they send is ignored). TEACHERS/admins filter explicitly by `standard_id`, `section_id`, `subject_id`. Each topic in the response includes `topic_content`, `image_url` and `pdf_url` (ready-to-use absolute URLs, or null).
- **Create / update / delete** — used by the teacher syllabus & content editors. Chapters are created one at a time (may start with no topics). Topics need only a `topic_name`; for Study Content also send `topic_content` and (as multipart) an `image` / `pdf`.

The single-item create endpoints (`POST /content/chapter`, `POST /content/topic`) were added for the app — distinct from the heavier `POST /content/upload` bulk importer. Auth: Sanctum bearer token in `authToken`.

request.description:
Paginated chapters (each with its `topics[]`), ordered by `order` then `id`. Every topic carries `topic_name`, `topic_content`, `link`, `image_url` and `pdf_url` (absolute URLs or null) + `order`.

- **Teacher/admin:** send `standard_id`, `section_id`, `subject_id` to scope to one class+subject.
- **Student:** the server forces the scope to the student's own class+section — just send `subject_id` (other filters are ignored). Returns 404 if the student record is missing.

Create a single chapter (no topics required). Idempotent on (organization, standard, section, subject, name) — re-sending the same name returns the existing chapter. `order` (integer, default 0) controls sort position; `description` is optional. The app's syllabus editor sends just `name` + `order`. Returns the chapter with an empty/loaded `topics[]`.
Update a chapter's name + description. Scoped to the caller's organization; 404 if not found.
Delete a chapter and all of its topics (and any associated topic files). Scoped to the caller's organization; 404 if not found.

Create a single topic under an existing chapter. The Syllabus editor sends just `topic_name` + `order` (integer, default 0). For Study Content, also send `topic_content`, `link` (any URL) and optionally an `image` (jpeg/png/jpg/gif, max 4MB) and/or `pdf` (max 10MB) — when a file is attached send the request as multipart/form-data instead of JSON. The response topic includes `image_url` / `pdf_url` / `link`. The chapter must belong to the caller's organization; 404 otherwise.

Update a topic — also the endpoint the app's Study Content editor uses to add material INTO an existing topic: `topic_content` (text), `link` (any URL — web link / doc / video), `order`, and a new `image` / `pdf` sent as multipart (replaces the existing file, old one deleted). Only fields that are sent are changed. Response includes the refreshed `image_url` / `pdf_url` and `link`. Scoped to the caller's organization via the parent chapter; 404 if not found.
Delete a single topic (and any associated files). Scoped to the caller's organization via the parent chapter; 404 if not found.

Heavier bulk importer: create multiple chapters each with multiple topics in one call. Unlike the syllabus Create Topic endpoint, here every topic requires `topic_content`; topics may also carry an `image` / `pdf` file (send as multipart in that case).
